Very weak. This app does not do anything that you can't get for free on other apps. It's totally glitchy - part way through exercises you'll get a 'something went wrong' message and have to restart. There's an annoying narrator that constantly says 'just hang on a little longer'. And when you don't get results, good luck getting a refund. My advice: run, don't walk, to alternatives that are just as good or better for little or no cost.

Beware! The app consumes enormous amount of traffic. For example, a 40 min workout takes up to 300 MB. If you are on a limited data plan through a mobile network, you may want to take this into account. I'm not sure if the app is poorly optimized or if it contains a hidden malware that utilizes a smartphone as an access point to convey extra data not related to the app itself.

This app is a total scam. No explanation on the exercises they propose you to do, just a single image with the name of the move but if you don't know what it is...you're on your own. No way either to actually track the progress of the series of exercises, whether you completed them or not. No link to fitness monitor to track calories either...not sure why it costs 60 bucks/year. I feel utterly stupid. I want my money back

PSA.. This app, I'm sure, is great, but don't do what I did and get the subscription for 90 days and not pay attention to the receipt. This auto renews in 90 days at a higher price than my intro amount. I only used it a couple of times and forgot about it due to a hectic work/life schedule and only realized it auto renewed when I received a notice from my bank. They will not refund the amount unless you cancel at least one day before it's processed. This is a lesson learned on my part.

The app works well with music apps & I am enjoying some of the add on sessions to the regular workouts. I think the avatar that shows you how to do some of the exercises is really cool. I am down 16 lbs in 90 days, just bought another 90 days. 1 negative is the pricing. I canceled my membership & got 60% off to stay, otherwise I would not have rejoined. Why not just offer that best price ? I know why, it's just seems a bit underhanded, especially to an existing customer. Cool workouts though!

Good app but if it crashes, you cannot start from where you left off. It does not allow you to go back one step. Also the step counter is pretty much usless without a clunky phone in your pocket. Would be great if it integrated with a watch. That being said the workout routine works good for me. Used this app a bit more and I believe the " back one step" issue was with my mobile device and not the app. So I'm going to give this app 5 stars. Enjoying it.

Great workout app. I'm a runner, and I need something to help in other areas. This app is great, and the workouts are easy to follow. I'm not sure I've seen any actual results yet, but I'll keep doing it. I like the option of doing it at home, without equipment as gyms are not my thing. I do see what others have pointed out, though. Some exercises with lots of reps aren't given enough time to complete before moving on.

Pretty good app, buttttt very big butttt, If you accidentally closed the app it clears out your workout. Sometimes even when you have it set up to have a workout ready for you it doesn't. You can't add reps to already existing workouts. You can't add new exercises to your workout it's whatever it decides. The usability of this office very limited. I paid for reduced year probably will not renew when full price comes up. I would say try the app out but it's a lot more frustration then it's worth

Great app.. I was happy to see that it wasn't a pay to use app. I was able to exercise with it for about 3-4 days before it became a pay to use app. It was a bit sudden to me since I had just used it the day before (a few hours before) and it was free but I wasn't upset. I recommend keeping track of the exercises if you don't wanna pay for the app so that you can exercise even when your free trial runs out. I recommend.

App was great when it worked. I was overcharged, then the app stopped working. I was told the one month introductory charge would be $10. But when I signed up I was charged $30. I decided to try it anyway. The app was great while it lasted. Then after 2.5 weeks the app stopped working. I logged out to see if that would help, but then I could t log in. I have contacted customer support, but they have been no help.

Scam! I bought the app on Friday per 6 months expecting videos of how to do each exercise but it didn't. I explain to them that as native Spanish speaker searching in Google how to do each exercise for the next 6 months didn't work. I offered them to split cost and asking for 50% refund of what I paid 72 hours after the purchase. 5 minutes after I sent them an email asking for that they answered it saying that " after review my case" they can't. Good luck with that model of business!

I started the Muscle Booster work out plan to be more fit as I get older. I've been at it for 3 weeks now and I feel good. The warm-ups before workout and the cool down period after are great. What I like is that you can add challenges to your basic plan and if you don't like something in the routine whether it be too easy, hard or boring....you can change it. I would definitely recommend this app'

I've enjoyed this app, over 8 months of usage, I'd like to see 2 things. Ability to record lifted weight, so the next time that workout comes up, I can see what I did last. The other thing I'd recommend is adding the option to change rest period. Sometimes I don't want 90 seconds rest between sets. (Yes, I know I can just skip ahead after desired rest time) Edit: 10/24/2023 Thank you for taking my suggestion and implementing it. I love that I can add my weight directly into the sets.

They make discounted offers on their website during initial sign-in. If you try to add that to your purchase, you get a busy icon until the offer times out. Upon contacting customer support, they say the offer may come up again in the future. That's what they call resolved. I cancelled immediately and will file a Paypal dispute if I don't get a refund.

Not a bad app. I have used it now for lifting for a good amount of time with the paid version. The guide and short videos on proper form are very helpful for me. What I don't like is that I can't move workouts around on a given week. IE - my gym lift days are Tuesdays and Fridays. As such I have to move up and back days on the calendar to be able to view those workouts. I'd much rather get a list of workouts for that week and then select them as I go through the week.

Before purchasing this app, i saw many ads about this application. On the ads, it shows you can choose any part of your body and the training video/exercises will show you how to use the tools. I paid full amount of money and very disappointed. What ever you choose its not same as the ads. I asked to get refunds but nothing happened. Almost a year i have this app but never use it. There is a lot of free app is better than this one. Do not waste your time/ money.

At first I loved the app, I paid for the full version, great app for workouts. But now it sucks, it keeps deleting my workouts that I've done, it doesn't let me input it for past days. And also when registering for weight it doesn't go past 99lb for workouts, that's doesn't make any sense. Please fix the issues.

The app wont work. At all. I had forgotten about the subscription because I never got to use the app in the first place and got charged $96 when it did an automatic renewal I thought I had canceled because the app is useless. Stuck on the very first loading screen. And it's been like that since I first downloaded it. Complete waste of my money. I could have just saved that total of $130ish dollars for something that either actually worked or something more important.

Used to be great to build workouts. But now they made it a subscription. I had purchased the lifetime one prior which was very reasonable priced. They then stated that it was for the meal plan. In which I can't even use that without needing another subscription. So I am just giving them up and going with an alternative app.

Well I am liking the app. I'm moving parts of my body that I don't think I've moved and exercised for years. Some exercises are a little difficult but they're good for me cuz I'm moving those parts of my body for now. Light exercise I believe is good for me and maybe later I'll increase difficulty. PS seems to be a good app. You seem to care and that's important to me. Sincerely, Sam PS some exercises I don't know if I did properly, but even if I haven't just moving helps me.

great app, the only thing I wished they had was the ability to say how sore your muscles are. I've had it where it will say a certain part of my body is at 100%, but it really is only at like 70%. I like that they show you what worked in the workout, but I wish that in the plan creating section, it would allow you to add that information. Besides that, it's an awesome app. It's really good for all lev3ls of working out!

The app used to be better with diverse and adequate sets. Now I have issues: 1. With "morning routine" the sound (beeps and "go") goes off for some sets in the middle of exercise and goes on again. 2. Some sets are not adequate e.g. "knee push ups" instead of full for advanced user, awkward sets such as "push up on forearms", there have been absurd sets e.g. "full planche" or "handstand walk" for which you need be be a champion to perform 3. Sets are repetitive, less diverse

Overall the app is pretty good. I have been fianlly using it for the last 6 months. The only issue I have and I hope the developer reads this and fix it is that when I try to change a specific exercise I don't like, it let's me pick a different one but when going back to the full routine the alternate exercise I picked was not taken but if I try to go and pick it again it is no longer available. This happens like 50% of the times.

BEWARE! Cool app. But since I forgot to cancel, and had NO REMINDER, I was charged $38.95 for my 2nd month (after a $15.95 1st month). After I cancelled, then I got two more "discounted" offers for subsequent months- too late, I got suckered for the 2nd month, so I feel fugged and am done with this app, which I otherwise like quite a bit. I will not pay $39.00 a month for it- BEWARE!

DO NOT DOWNLOAD THIS APP This app used to be great before they pay-walled it. And it's not even the pay wall that makes it terrible now. The workouts used to seem tailored by a professional, like a real-life CrossFit instructor set them up. Now it's just a mismatch of AI-generated workouts. IE, you want to target the chest? Well here's 8 chest exercises, 7 of them target the same muscles and there's no break up in between. this app is more likely to hurt you than help

Pretty decent app 3.5 stars. Needs to be able to put in the weight we are lifting next to each exercise. So we can keep track week over week. Also there is no way to go back and record a workout you did but didn't track that day. Also needs a monthly view showing all the workouts you did not just weekly. If it was a free app I'd give 5 stars but if I'm paying I expect a few more basic features *edit* they have updated some of the features I mentioned in the review to make the app better

I really enjoy this app. I rated a 4 star because I believe it has a lot of room to grow. For starters, I think the "plans" under main activity could be more customizable. Theres a workout creator which I really like but I think that could be implemented with your plan for the entire week. I already have my gym routines and I'm starting to plateau, so I was looking for new exercises and this app has done that. Just hope they keep building on it.

Just finished my third workout. So far so good. I mainly got the app because I was failing on my own to keep up with workouts and mixing up my workouts to maximize my effort. I love how it asks after you're done with a workout how easy it was. Your answer will help the app tailor your next workout. With this app I don't have to think anymore. Just do. Again, so far so good.

I purchased the annual subscription for this app. The download and set up went well. I used the planner tab and when I completed the daily workout it would not show complete and would not allow the next workout to begin because it didn't show the previous workout as completed. Basically stuck and useless. I could use the create workout but not the 30 day planner. Response from customer service was prompt but they could not resolve the issue. I canceled the subscription.

Great for overall workouts, reminders, decent meal ideas, etc. However, there could be some quality of life updates. When using dumbells or free weights, it doesn't state if it is weight per dumbell or total. Also, after selecting specific areas I want to work on i.e. abs, it will throw in back and shoulder workouts without being able to change with a different set without just skipping it entirely.

I know that most workouts are already available online, but the simplicity and convenience of this app definitely shook up my routine to the point that I am feeling sore again! But the UI is lacking. - Need to be able to go directly to a specific exercise in that day's workout by clicking from the list, rather than having to skip repeatedly - Please implement "delay" function, where you can push your workout routine by a day without skipping over the whole workout and body part.

Bare bones for the price tag but the big issue is that you pay for it and it asks for ratings every time you open it no matter how many times you give a rating. HOW ARE YOU ENJOYING THE APP!? HOW LIKELY ARE YOU UP RECOMMEND TO A FRIEND!? When you pay for something like this, it should be all about function! I shouldn't have to pat the developer on the back and reassure them with feedback to the point that it impedes my ability to quickly utilize the thing I paid for. Real dumb.

Do not subscribe to this app! I started my subscription 2 days ago, so I thought. I was charged $25, and I downloaded the app only to find out they want more money $$ in subscriptions. All I got for my money was 12 weeks of a pedometer, and that is it. No access to any workout routines and not way to communicate with anyone just canned responses and no way for a refund. Horrible customer service and a money grab! Update They have worked with me to resolve the problem. Now I love using this app.

Horrible, wasted $30, read the reviews before purchase. Woke up excited to try it and the work out was horrible, picked the gym option and it had me doing arm swings and marching in place. Like really, I want to use weights that's why I go to the gym. No ability to customize work out or track what weights are being used. The meal plan is a joke, there is zero planning and the meals are ridiculous unless you have time to prep an hour each meal and shop multiple items that don't get used in other.

I've been using this app for some time on and off. Now that I'm sticking to my workout plan I can definitely say that this app fkn rocks. Light to heavy workouts, however you like to workout. Daily tips to fitness that include informational articles that don't include fluff, just facts. I personally think the yearly price is a bit cheap since I don't pay a gym payment but either way don't let the cheap yearly price steer you to other apps "Promising gains" at a high price.

Great app! Two things I would suggest: Tracking items such as weight, calories, water, etc., and the ability to schedule custom workouts so you can repeat them. other than that, very good app. Been using it for six months and have seen some impressive changes (for me). I was new to working out and this was the app I chose to start my journey. it has been worth it.

Exercises aren't accurate--half don't reflect the images, the other half don't describe what the exercise should do. The 'AI' is unable to help unless it's the last routine (not saved). The timers are infuriating and you can't switch to reps on timed exercises. And support doesn't respond to any requests for help or to fix things. Not even a good workout app.

I liked the app for the most part in the beginning. I created my own workout and no major issues. using it for about 4 months then all of a sudden my routines dissappear... no idea why. I went into the Ai feature to see if I could write up a new plan... I picked a muscle group that I want to work, say back workout, Ai gives me legs and chest... very disappointed wish I could get my money back!

was ok. it was having technical issues. I signed up for the year but had to cancel. I only had it for a few hours. I'd like a refund please. reached out over email — pending review. I hope this doesn't deter anyone from trying the app. It looks good with a lot of options and functions, examples and even a recovery map. My phone just wasn't having it. Foldable Phones guys... foldable... phones ....

("Contact Us" doesn't work in the app) Bought a 1 year subscription, then I cancelled it just so it wouldn't automatically renew next year. Now I get constant pop ups and a permanent icon covering the workout information that is offering me a discounted subscription. I am subscribed, I shouldn't have to deal with these things just because it's not on automatic renewal!

I like the workout routines the AI comes up with, and the fact that I can add, remove, and replace exercises whenever I want. I really like that it shows you how to perform each exercise, as well. I also like that it keeps track of my workouts, which muscles I'm using, and my body measurements. There was an earlier review saying that if you switched screens while a workout was in progress, it would restart your progress when you switched back, but that bug seems to be fixed.

Good app - but PLEASE PLEASE PLEASE let me have the opportunity to keep NOTES on my workouts. If I use resistance bands, I have NO WAY to put in how much resistance I used on a given exercise. I need to be able to keep notes on my workouts, like "Stay at this weight" or "GO DOWN next time" or "GO UP next time." I can't do that in the app and it's a VERY limiting factor for an all-in-one app for the gym.

The only "AI" feature is the exercise suggestions. Otherwise, you are basically just putting everything together yourself, but they only let you see 3 of the same different choices each time you want to add a new exercise, so you end up hitting "suggest a new one" a dozen times every single time. I was hoping for some help finding a workout despite my chronic pain barriers, but another app bites the dust. It also asks for a hefty subscription fee to use the AI chat. ChatGPT to the rescue...🙄😒

I'm enjoying the AI generated workouts and how you can change it up to make it more taylored to your needs. I also like how you can put your own workouts together based on what muscle groups or what equipment you want to use. It's exactly what I been looking for but I wish when entering the weight you used it gave more options like, .25lbs, .50lbs, .75lbs, as of right now you can only enter in whole numbers. It would 100% better for tracking purposes if this were and option.

NEEDS MORE FEATURES... I love the app but think it would be AWESOME if I could take notes and use the app to track my water intake, nutrition (meals, calories, fats, carbohydrates, protein), and the amount of sleep I'm getting. I'm currently using three different apps to track these items and it would be so much easier to track them all in one place.

This does not provide personalized workout. It is very limited and cannot take into account injuries. I was thinking that this would actually provide a personalized workout since I sprained my ankle and needed to learn new exercises; it cannot do that at all. This app is actually a lot less flexible than many other exercise apps. Also I wrote customer service 5 minutes after I bought the app and tested it out asking for a refund, and they will not refund the annual purchase.

I love this app! 4 stars only because if you switch screens during your workout, it restarts your time and doesn't allow you to change it. So after I'm done lifting weights and it's time for my cardio, I open up another app to watch some TV while I finish up, but my time restarts. So instead of it logging the hour+ that I'm working out, it only logs the time from when I bring the app to the front again, even though the timer on the app is showing over an hour. Fix this and definitely giving 5!

Hello. Today was day 1. Loved it. Perfect as I wanted it. I was looking for an easy app to use and to change while working out with the images of the body I was focusing on plus the excercises. I have alot of feedback as of right now from only after 1 session, but will try to learn the app over time. So far, great. I was searching for 1 month and looking at at least 5 apps with trials and didn't like any. This one seems to be exactly what I wanted. I will come back and grade this overtime!

The pros: good price, a good amount of exercises, good data keeping. The Cons: to look for workouts, you have to type them in specifically the way they have them, some common workouts are missing, the ai generated workouts feel are more like randomly generated and not really specific like other workouts apps. Overall, good app for beginners.

As a person where money is tight. I am looking for free apps..i dont mind ads...when i see and download apps that claim to be free. Maybe what they need to say is free for 3 days..after that you can look but zero interaction unless you pay up. Its irritating to try and open the app only for it to show a workout but you cannot open the workout..cannot interact..cannot progress. I wpuld not recommend this app based on the bait and switch

I tried out Fit AI for one session. However I discovered that it required a bench for many of the workouts - which I don't own - and it was difficult to find a workaround. I deleted the app without realizing that I failed to cancel the trial. I realized a week later when I was charged $106.99. I immediately emailed again and again, but they refused to respond to my request for a refund. THEY REPLIED HERE FOR THE LOOK, BUT STILL HAVE NOT RESPONDED TO MY EMAILS.

Searching exercises is a pain since you need to type the exact name that the app uses. So if you type barbell overhead press you will not see it in the search since the app calls it military press. You also can't look at other routines while you are currently in a workout, it will end the workout and then you have to start over. The AI feature is a gimmick and does not measure muscle wear at less than 10 RPE. I did 3 sets of crutches and the app thinks my abs are at 0%. Not worth paying for.

I'm a natural fit person, so I've never had much experience in gyms. This app definitely helps me get into learning routines and targeted muscle training. Absolutely gave me the confidence I needed to keep going to the gym. Also, this isn't a complaint, more so a suggestion. I would like to see flexibility/stretching or warm-ups added in the future. I think that would be an amazing feature for this app.

Overall, I like the app. It took me a few days to understand how the workouts were logged. I didn't realize I had to go through the whole routine and finish so all the workouts were logged together for the day. Also took me a while to realize I couldn't manually create my own workout and input the info (e.i. zumba, bands, etc.) I wish they had a user guide or Q&A section. The Ai doesn't answer any app function questions.

It has this 5-minute sale counter every time when opening it. Also, this 3-day trial advertisement can't be closed anymore :) They removed the close button. Therefore, I can not do anything in this app anymore. I used it almost a year now, and the concept was actually quite good and had potential. Too bad it's scammy and doesn't work anymore

I got a new phone and when I go to log in after downloading the app on a new phone, clicking the sign into account button at several different locations within the app does nothing. Can not use the app or my subscription I paid for. Update to developer response. I have already emailed with and your suggestion did not solve or fix the issue. Still can't log into the app. Clicking the button to log in does absolutely nothing.

wastage of money. 1st, I don't like the app interface for logging the reps. 2nd, no video source to see the correct form of exercise. 3rd, AI itself recommends some exercises that i cannot find in the exercise list and the search can be improved. 4th, it is not a one stop app i have to look over multiple sources for getting a proper routine. 5th, there could be additional knowledge about muscles and the exercises. there are more things which this app lacks but can't mention all.

It is really scam! I was charged twice. So I still had the subscription. Today I decided to cancel the suvscription , so they won't charge me for the next year. My current subscription is funushing in March 2027. Now when I am opening app , it straight away suggests me to subscribe and I can not use the app in full as I cancelled subscription for next year, but current subscription is active.DO NOT TRUST THEM!!!Will have to report them and will contact my bank for refund

the variety of exercises to do every workout really helps me stick with working out more often. and the way you can track which muscle groups your hitting harder then others so you know to focus on other groups is great. longest I kept with an app of any sorts, workout or not.

Don't like it , I choose this app after trying a few when I bought the premium I realized it has less features and Finished workouts keep disappearing i definitely wish I could only pay for a month used and get the rest of my money back , I did cancel in 2 days but I cant get a refund that suck because I stuck with it for a year

never recommend this app as i subscribed for first year with discounted price with no problem but for the second year I paid the full price more than double of first year and the app didn't activated so I paid for full year for nothing and after complain to customer service, I got nothing !

the free trial lasted 2 days before it started asking for my credit card. a workout app is something very personal and something you need time to figure out if it's for you. so not my jam and uninstalling and going back to Fiton and paying for a year. it's the best for the $$$

A great app. It would be great if the AI aspect would learn your routines, suggest new exercises based on where you're training (which instruments are available) and switch exercises from the chat window. Otherwise, there isn't really any AI involved IMOP

quite helpful the only issue it has is the same of all i find in all workout planner: only consider the muscles but not the muscle heads for the workouts. this way, even with app help, i need to find some professional to create my workout sessions.  Other sugestion would be the "model" be able to be watched from different angles not only one view. it would help a lot and be a plus for users.  anyway, the support was very useful when i needed.

waste of money,, half way through a workout the app stops and you have to start a second time to finish workout data,, you then have two different workouts... very frustrating,,, Paid for the year at a discount and even so a waste of money... Moved onto a different app with good reviews and a 14 day free trial... Feniangaafan.

I initially subscribed to them and paid a small fee to try out the app. I cancelled the app 2 months in advance however they still deducted a yearly amount from my account. now when I ask for refund there is no response. This app is a scam dont ever purchase anything on it.

Tries to trick you into purchasing a discounted plan immediately after setting up an account with a four minute deadline. you can't even sample the app before the shady stuff starts. Using urgency tactics to lure you in instead of just making a good app.

I love this app! I've been getting a great variety of exercises that might have been out of my comfort zone before and I am feeling it everywhere. And if I don't like one that pops in the AI replace button will suggest and replace it in my workout so I still get full body workout. love it!

need option 1.warm sets. 2.drop sets 3.normal sets 4. failure set and you want include RIR scale from easy to hard how was sets please include in app this option then be 5 stars WOW I comment like nearly 5 months ago need update to better version but is no improvement and you trying charge more then 100 Euro this is like feel's scam no updates no nothing and you know you need update to better version I love this app but is need get better just simple setting you need add
Excellent. Four stars until it can automatically generate a ready-to-log routine from the AI Chat, where I don't have to go find the suggested exercises myself and manually create a log routine.

Generally like it, but it doesnt hold all machines found in a typical gym. It also doesnt update age for so I am forever the age I was when I first started. A monthly weight check-in would be useful. Also would love if it suggested the right stretches for each exercise set rather than having to start a new plan or manually search. I love a 20 day plan rather than 3 day repeats, but it doesnt allow to merge plans or to easily add exercises from other saved plans.

There is no 7-day free trial available if you choose to pay for the subscription, as the subscription starts on the same day. This information is not clearly stated on their app it s 7 days trial . I canceled my subscription on the second day, but the full amount for a one-year subscription was still deducted from my account. Despite sending multiple emails to their support team, I have not received any response. I do not recommend this app.

Do not download! It's a scam! I wish I read the other reviews mentioning the same problem. Your forced to sign up to use the app, so I started the trial and immediately cancelled the subscription to avoid being charged and then I got charged!, contacted them for a refund and there's no help from them.. disappointing and unfair. Oh and there's a lot of 5 star bot reviews!

Neither the human anatomy nor the dumbbells, barbells and gym equipment to shape it change over time; the movements are the same since people started working out. All a fitness app can do is vary the sequence of exercises and the number of repetitions a little -- which is too simple a functionality to justify bleeding customers dry with recurring subscription fees ad infinitum. A one-time purchase would be o.k., but competitor apps (or books) can be bought instead of rented. Uninstalled.

Good app with some flaws... The AI Feature isn't really useful imo, but I like the way an exercise can be replaced by another one, which works like a charm. On the other hand, it's not possible to go back and view other exercises, e.g. during a break, because this cancels the whole workout. The biggest flaw is, that the workouts cannot be synced with other Fitness Apps, such as Google Fit. I would REALLY apprechiate that feature...

I would say overpriced. The chatgpt feature is a gimmick. The lack of integration with wearable devices and with health apps is a bit of a joke given that competing apps have this with a lower price point. The lack of a voice prompt is another obvious miss. I do like the exercise list, and the ease with which you can sub out one you don't want to do. It is great that there are videos demonstrating the exercises. All said, this has a ways to go before being a truly premium app.

I've been using the free version of this app for a few months now and I really like the app and it's functions even for a free version but lately I haven't been able to use it. After you click start workout it pops up with the 2 pro version popups and after closing those it closes the workout to. Hope it's an easy fix cause I really enjoy the app otherwise.
I was first skeptical about this app. regardless I paid for the basic and kick-started . My core muscles cries in pain, sweat profusely within a short time span and my body is getting so well toned.

Even after paying for yearly subscription, the app keeps on asking to upgrade to pro. Do not purchase this app at all. I have lodged a complaint with google with the receipt of payment. Also, when you tap contact us, there is nothing, no email, no phone number. So, this app is basically to dupe people's money. I will make sure that this app is removed from google play store.
Hello. Why did you charge £11.99 from my PayPal account when I canceled my subscription the next day? You offered a 3-day free trial. Please refund my money immediately.
Amazing fitness app. Simple, effective, and free! this is the best fitness app i've ever used
I use this app for a while now. I really like the variety of exercises. It was exactly what I was looking for as I needed to change my exercises

first they proposed 80% discount I was thinking to maybe try it. but cancelled the subscription before trial ended and they penalty me with 70% discount now. guys that is not motivating me to buy it. if you want it this way - giving you 1 star and waiting for 90 % discount, how bout that?
I love being able to track all my workouts with this and I really enjoy how I can pick or AI will if I want different options. it's a really great without partner👍🏼

It forces you to pay...after the first workout you can see the second but it won't register and once you try to do so, it pops up the free trial and you can't register your workout, neither can see the others, the app is great, but too expensive, it's an AI not a real personal trainer, which is cheaper than the app in my country, I got the app exactly to not hire one lol
Great app. I wish you could add custom exercises that aren't in the exercise library.
seems awesome so far!! this app has exactly what I was looking for. I can choose each muscle and what machine I want to use. it's perfect!!
good app, helpful for beginners. only issue app crashes mod workout more than once.
Don't ask me a lot of questions and then shove a subscription in my face. Warn us first and I want to test drive something before I buy. Ask after like a week or something.
Would be better if actual exercise videos were there. Also, it should suggest plans and excerise changes with time and progress, which it doesn't. Still needs work

update: per their request below, i emailed them for the third time. ----- original post: I signed up with a 3 day free trial ; however, I was charged for the entire month that very same day. I contacted them via email and it appears that they're refusing to refund me my money.... this is a SCAM.
I love this app and I just wish they'd have a voice command tools or options and also like a voice prompt for time and etc.
Awesome app. modify workout to target specific areas. Tracks muscle gain and results, shows all muscles being workes with each exercise.
Great app. Just wish there was a function that allowed you to filter the exercises based on difficulty level.
I got charged a weekly amount even though there is a 3 day free trial. I cancelled my subscription. This is unethical.
Its functionality is limited, you cannot return to the program you were working on, and it is not user-friendly. It's useless.

It is really cost effective when compared to other apps that require a subscription. Love the fact that I can change the programs to my lifestyle. Takes some time to set up, but once done... you have a plan to work on for a while before being able to change in upping your game. It is simplistic and practical. Other apps have too many bells and whistles where here, I can focus on a routine that suits my needs and changes as life changes. Well done!

I purchased the app for 1 year, and everything was working fine. Suddenly, without any warning, the application went back to trial mode and asked me to pay again for something that I still have almost 1 year of use for. I sent an email to support about the problem with evidence of my purchase but there has been no response. Lack of respect.

Good App, enjoy the functionality of being able to customise workouts as you go, and the demonstration of how to do the exercise with correct form is useful. One thing I cannot find is a place where I can record general comments from the workout, eg. How you were feeling that day, or why you swopped out an exercise etc. Please assist?

Im using the premium features and it's not better than any other fit app out there. It sure tracks your reps, the weight you lift and suggests exercises to work different muscles and not repeat the same thing over and over, however it creates only 3 types of routines: build muscle, tone or lose weight and in neither of those it includes recuperation or stretching, mobility or flexibility. It would be great to add time available for workouts as a parameter too.

The unpaid version of the app just stopped working after about 3 months. I can still edit routines, but there is no way to log workouts. It goes straight to the paywall. There is no mention of a limited time to use the unpaid version. I tried to use just the unpaid features, but it seems that the moderators or the supposed AI blocks users from logging workouts.

A gimmick. I used the pro version for 1 month. During this time I found the workouts targetting muscle groups to be inconsistent, and often times not enough. Honestly not worth it. I opted for an app that let's me import and key in programmes I found online for free. The gpt chat was pointless and no better than what you get with the chatgpt official app.

I've been using this app for months and I don't need anything else for what I do. I travel a lot and I don't always have the same equipment or a gym available. - i can generate workout based on the muscles rest conditions - i can select what kind of equipment I want to use dynamically and it generates workouts based on that - it has statistics and integrated AI, although it would be great if the chat can be better integrated with the app to fetch data and generate exercises from my prompt

In the app, while purchasing yearly plan, they show offer banner valid for 5 min, for the price 999 INR for 1 year, but while doing the payment using gpay, its asking full amount 4999 INR. Why are you showing such false information, is that offer cannot be applied? Also when I clicked on Contact Us, to share this feedback. It doesn't show any text box, but just an info text "select something to share" and not allowing customers to enter anything.

The best Fitness App I've used. I love the visuallisation of the muscle group, and the recovery percentage. It looks really cool. Plus all sorts of workouts, bodyweight, full gym or what ever tools you have. Also you can add your runs, sprint, stretches to capture all your workouts, so you can see your body and muscles %. It would be great if, you can improve the following to make it even better: 1. Add adjustable timer countdown, including sound 2. Able to copy time and reps for each sets.
Worst!!!! They say there's a free trial of 3 days but then they charged you since the first day of sign in. Pls give me my money back 😡

once you get the customized workouts it's impossible to actually start the workout. the ads for the trial and payment keep popping up. I wouldn't mind if once you close them they go away but they don't and then you can't even start your workout! cost is too high for me to pay.

Best fitness app for me, so far! If I don't have equipment or if I want to do a different exercise, same muscle group, app provides SEVERAL alternatives. The timer/rep count/logging is in one place, so logging is simple & quick. Previously saved exercises with weight/reps is saved and auto populates when you repeat the same exercise in a different session. First program generated for me was perfect considering time, goal, experience & limitations 🥹. Not sure if the app has HIIT workouts. ❤️

Subscription auto renewed today despite not even being able to access the full app for the last few months (assumed Subscription had already ended) and now can't cancel anywhere in app and still can't access full app either! Have emailed through app but no response yet. Can somebody please remove the £94.99 pending transaction from my account please as this has caused me financial difficulty!

I have a regular gym goer over had a year off and starting again feel like a beginner and before I downloaded this I found myself in a very lost and confused plays without knowing where to start from. This app felt like someone got me by the hand and instructed me step by step. It's incredibly good for only a small amount of money yearly. Luckily I got the £25 per year deal instead of £94 or no way i would have installed it. It even let's you replace exercises but you don't like using AI. 👌
I paid for a year. After 6 months the app is blocked, I should pay more to continue. No communication works. I do not know how to solve.
paid for service, but unless you keep app open the entire time the rest periods dont alert you.
I'll give reasonable answers when I have done some exercises and the body shape is well excellent 👌

The worst. Hard to navigate. impossible to edit or change up routines. 100% impossible to cancel after trial. I have sent several emails to the developers and I have yet to hear back. At $99 this is crazy! I'd like a refund. Can some one please respond and process!!!! It's been months!!!!!
too much haye in descriptions and the the keyboard blocks the whole box if you wanted to type in it yourself
I cancelled my subscription after 1 week and there is no refund as they give me wrong program like 🙃 nothing I need my refund I pay 380 aed for nothing
You cannot test the platform without paying or see different workouts to know if this app is good for you or no.

I subscribed for a month, and here is my feedback. Pros: 1- Logs your workout nicely, but it is not the only app that does so. 2- It provides a somewhat decent library of exercises 3- It highlights the percentage by which each muscle is targeted per exercise Cons: 1- The muscle groups targeted are inconsistent. On a shoulder day, you get zero shoulder exercises and many back ones. 2- AI assistant is of no added value 3- It recommends exercises for muscles that are not recovered.

The best app for gym routines. They made a great exercise collection. I was looking for the app that will allow me to create routines and will have all the exercises I need. I tried every single app on play market and I can say that this app have the largest exercise collection (3700 against average 300-500). And every single exercise has video 🤯 Also love that recover feature, super useful. And overall app design is beautiful and practical! Bought the yearly subscription, it's definitely worth

Auto planning is great and you can replace each exercise if you don't have necessary equipment or want to do a different exercise targeting the same muscle groups. But there is no way to manually enter workout information or edit a finished workout. So to track your stats you need to be chained to your phone during a workout, mark the sets while the app wants to time each exercise and hope the workout timer stays correct. That's not for everyone and neither for me.

Essentially asks a couple of extremely basic questions, then suggests a bunch of seemingly entirely random exercises. While I'm trying to figure out if there's any way to get it to configure it with the exercises I actually want to do, gives me a fomo pop-up about a lifetime subscription that expires in 5 minutes. No discernibly useful functionality here.
What we all need, even if support isn't really responsive and the app has a few bugs here and there.
excellent application..it has everything you need to organize training..a good reminder and a visually beautiful display of exercises

It only let's you do the first day of the workouts unless you go Pro. You can't create any plans, add any excercises other than pushups or pullups, and can't even use the Ai generator. That is not a bad thing on its own, just it's annoying that they say that can be used for "free" when it's not. I would have never bothered downloading it in the first place.

I love how it helps me to choose training sequence according to my need. Instructions are easy to follow with personal weigh lifting exercises come in various ways. There are options for type of exercises, either for beginner or for pro. Also have inside timer and stopwatch to help measure each exercise we take. So far I've been subscribe for 3 years. Oh if you cancel subscription, they will offer you great discounted price.
Love it! Could include some HIIT workouts in empty workout options.
There are very good workouts in it and I got a lot of benefit from these workouts.

EDIT: I figured out how to adjust our workout plan, went to sign up and YIKES - they seem to raise the price of entry as days go on (over 30% since I installed). My original review stands-great app with an annoying and increasingly expensive nag screen. /// The app successfully walked us thru our first time back to the gym - it allowed me to add warmup and cooldown exercises for each day and add to the basic three day routine I chose as the template to work from. No errors or discrepancies.

I set my workout duration to 30 minutes, but the AI creator still generates only one 25‑minute cardio session and then adds a bunch of strength exercises, which makes the total workout almost 1 hour. The user interface also isn’t very intuitive or user‑friendly. It would be great if the app could better respect the selected workout time and improve the overall UI experience.

I want to go to the gym regulair, I want to make progress, but i don't care / no interest in what muscle i train today. This app gives me everyday good exercises. you see for every excercises when you last did that one, how many reps and the weight, very ease in use! for me the best app, because it gives me what i need. to reach 5 star from me: make a good website version so check the provided excercises on a browser version iso a small smartphone screen!

I like the app so far, but there is one real pain that should be easy to fix: when the exercise is based on time, you have to manually start a timer beside the exercise... It's very bothersome to do for every single set... I wish there was a way to have a timer start automatically for every set with bells when starting and done

Never done a review before but this app has done it. I thought it was cool at first and was able to do one workout ... one 40 minute workout. Thats it, after you do one workout they lock out of the rest of the workouts unless you pay 134 dollars a year for things you can search up on your own. Dont pay for this app dont even install, waste of time, waste of space you're better spending the money you would on this app for a gym membership and get better results

It's not bad. Makes no sense that it has stretching as options to load manually l, but the AI doesn't put pre-workout stretching into your routine for the muscles you are working that day. The point is to have a workout plan put together by AI, and it's missing the most important parts of the process.

It might as well just be glorified exercise encyclopedia. It isn't even considered as a planner when the functions are locked behind a pay wall. I thought that it was cool at first that this app would help plan our routines for the week but I can't even use the basic functions after day one. I don't recommend this app if it can't make the most basic functions of help us plan our routines.

Functionality that all logged exercises are deleted if you forget to tap Finish button is very user unfriendly in my opinion. App didn't save my progress, and customer care doesn't exist to help. I think there is much cheaper and better apps than this one. I am really sorry I bought one year subscription, I wouldn't do it again.

The suggested plan makes no sense, exercises repeat on the same session, replacement are not saved for the following week. somehow in a 5 days plan, some muscle groups are missed out completely. I contacted the support, heard nothing back. I'm always happy to pay for a good app but this is the most useless wasted money I ever spent on an app 😞

I bought the 6-month Premium plan, but requested a refund the same day after trying the app. The workouts felt unrealistic, too many exercises in too little time, constantly moving across the gym, and no clear structure. Even the custom workout generated random movements instead of a balanced plan. I wouldn't recommend paying for a long-term subscription. Try one month first to see if it fits your needs.

it's alright. app works as intended and has good workouts. i forget easily so i turned on push-notifications but I'm unable to change what time i get them, is it really so popular to workout before dinner? I'm always busy with things around the house so it's useless at this time in the end but there's no place I could find to change it. I'm also finding that the workouts given to me daily are 80% stretching and not actually working out, even after clicking the option that I am not satisfied.

Definitely read the reviews before purchasing! I felt all of the reviews were quite accurate and the app is great or horrible depending on the user. In most cases so is working out. Working out and finding the right resources to make it easier is complicated. This app is a bit funny in how it shows its interface. A woman discusses kegal exercises and another person discuss the illegitimate use of male body builders. Use for pre-workout or for someone who doesn't know how/where to start !

After subscribing to this app, I purchased a Samsung Galaxy Tab S10+ so I could use the app on larger screen. There is no way to log into the app using the subscription I already paid for. It requires me to go through the setup process again, only to get to a point where it tells me that I already have a subscription. But then offers no way for me to get into that subscription. Extremely disappointed that the exercise animations are just as tiny on a tablet as they are on the cell phone.

I've been using the for a few weeks now, and it's been a game-changer for my fitness routine. The app offers a wide range of workout plans, from beginner to advanced, making it suitable for all levels. The exercise demonstrations are clear and easy to follow, ensuring I maintain proper form. I also love the progress tracking feature, which helps me stay motivated.

Your literally cannot do anything without paying. Not even see the exercises with ads or something. Moreover they ask that you leave a 5* review without seeing any of the workouts saying you'll receive a 7-day premium once you do so but then you have to send a screenshot of the review to their email first. Why can't you keep things simple?

App is good, a couple of points that the team should handle. If home workout is considered,please remove the bench exercise, one might not have benches at home. The stretching excercise should be improved, they are not merely excercise, they help to relax the muscles, some instructions can be added of what is the correct way of doing it. improve the setting of streaks, as they start the counting again after rest days.

The "in-app purchase" is deceptive because there's no free program, it's effectively a paid app. The developers gave me a code for a free trial, and I can say it's well done, offer a massive range of options and very flexible. Customer support is also one of the best I've seen (reaction time and details in replies).

Dogpiss AI app that's so buggy it's unreal. Workouts are not what they are titled, you'd see one thing then open it it's something completely different. 'Dumbell midline burn' but has 4 exercises with 'lat pulldown machine', what's even worse there's 5 different machines required for a workout that trains same exact muscle. Just stay away from this app, you will not get to try it out before paying and they will not refund your money. ChatGPT could create a more sensible workout.

This app isn't great for beginners, it's a lot of work to make custom workouts, It doesn't save workout equipment for daily use, and there aren't any real workout programs, especially for beginners. On the first day of the first week, I got V-up Abs with dumbbells, and when I chose custom with AI, I got the treadmill between each exercise. I definitely regret the annual subscription.

I demand an immediate cancellation of my subscription & disputing the charges made by Muscle Monster and have escalated this matter to the Google Play Store team. The app charged my card amounts that I consider excessive and unexpected. The automatic renewal terms and the "no refund" policy were not presented in a clear, prominent, and transparent manner at the point of subscription. The renewal fee was several times higher than the amount I originally paid, without adequate notice

playStoreReviews.text: This is a great app. My only issue is that it will drain the phone's battery in about 2 hours if the screen is always on. Is there a "night mode" that would use less energy? (Solution: skip changing from Power Saving mode.) Question: Why does the calorie counter begin dropping as my speed drops? My effort is greater climbing enen though my speed is lower. Conversely, when I'm coasting down a hill, the calorie counter adds calories burned. This is a bug, not a feature.

Some time in the past I really enjoy map my ride. I start my ride walk or run and everything is tracked. When I'm done I save my workout and it all goes to my account for access on my computer.. the monthly updates put the month into perspective. My only problem is when I lose the GPS signal, most tracking becomes meaningless. Not too often, but it happens. Setting my routes hasn't worked out too well. But 90% works and works great. I use out too the max

This cycling tracking app is simple and easy to use. It tracks your rides and distance well without being confusing, which makes it great for quick use. The layout is clean and straightforward, so you can start riding without messing with settings. It covers the basics, but I couldn’t find a calories burned feature. Overall, it’s a solid app if you want something simple and reliable for tracking your bike rides.

I love the data it gives me as I'm riding, however I noticed that the current speed is typically off by 4 to 8 mph. I have a speedometer on my bike and then I also cross-check it across two different odometer apps which all reflect the same speed but the app for map my ride is often like I said 4 to 8 me less which unfortunately affects your calorie counting estimation.

I don't have a fit bit or anything like it, because map my ride does everything I want, I use it for walking, hiking, and biking. I just had a hip replacement, and I plan to use it to track my progress. It is very flexible while staying unobtrusive, and it has all of the tracking I want and is quite accurate.

I downloaded this app as well as mapmywalk to record my walking and riding distance. I do not need the completely unnecessary notifications so I have them turned off. Now everytime I open Mapmyride I get a message saying notifications are off. If I don't want the notifications I also don't want messages about them. Oh and I switched back to an older phone and I could see my progress by pulling down the menu bar So after a few updates that is gone. Why? I am doing other things with my phone.

Worked when I bought it, now its a glorified timer. Even went online and mapped out the route, still can't track it accurately. So other than time, I get no accurate data. I thought it was because I live in China, but other people use western tracking apps that have no issues. Time to switch apps I guess.

For 2 weeks now Map My Ride has been unreliable, stopping during my walks and bike rides, where it barely tracks any of the ride/walk anymore. I've uninstalled/reinstalled, updated the app, and nothing works. Anyone experiencing this issue? This was my go-to app but I'll have to use something else without a fix. Easy to use, I can follow friends, there are options for cycling and walking, and I love that it keeps my stats. I also like that I can save each activity with a description and image!

It started with the upsells almost immediately. This just a gateway store for athleticwear. The app is unintuitive. I can see spending a few minutes signing up so it keeps a record. First ride? No easy access to the ride route history. That's an upsell. Criminy, just don't even get me started. If you can't offer me at least a minimum of usefulness free forever, well there's no reason to leave the app on the phone. Goodbye.

I was initially happy with this app, but then they added lots of new paid features that get in the way of a simple workout. The last straw was when I completed my workouts it has now started to prompt me to save my workout, where it never did before. I click save and it takes me to some page where I have to enter a name and a description. This is complete nonsense! When I go back to the end of workout page, there is no way around the prompt, forcing me to abandon the workout. Fine. Uninstalled.

I seem to have nothing but trouble with this app. I used it for a long time without issue, but then I would get caught in a Save Workout loop. Even after selecting to discard the workout it would just take me back to the Save Workout window. The only way out is to uninstall and reinstall. More recently I'm getting to my destination only to find that it stopped tracking about halfway, making it pointless to record the rides. Today I'm back in save workout loop and have once again uninstalled.

Decent cycling app! Fairly easy to use. Records the necessary info for each ride. Allows each workout to be edited, which is very useful. I subtracted 1 star because it does not record each month's mileage separately. It will document rides by weeks & overall mileage, but not the total for each month. I like to compare month to month without having to use a calculator. If I'm missing something, please let me know. Thanks!

The only really annoying thing is that in terrain view when you zoom it in to be able to read the numbers on the contour lines, the contour lines go away. When the contours are visible the contour numbers are not. Also, it would be nice if the elevations (feet) in the elevation profile we're round numbers. Perhaps they would be round numbers if they were in meters. I haven't checked that.

I have used this app for 4ish years now, and it was always pretty reliable. But recently (2025) it keeps stopping mid-workout. Easily 4/5 of my bike rides end with me finding the app stopped miles ago. My phone hardware, software, and even my route hasnt changed. The app gives you a lot of great features, but sadly they are meaningless if the basic function fails. In its current state, i cannot recommencd if you are a user who wants a semi-accurate record.

Perfect app, works great... BUT there is a problem with the audio saying "workout started" and "workout finished" while the person's phone is silenced. It's kind of awkward when I finish my bike ride, go indoors and hold the button down to hear it say that, loudly, in front of family. Aside from that, I would recommend a way to search for an address or location using the app, instead of using Google Maps.

I hadn't used the app for a number of months. When I resumed, the "current speed" indicator had gone wonky. I can be heading down a hill, quickly gaining speed - with the "average speed" showing a small increase as well - but the "current speed" indicator shows a sustained and steady DECREASE in speed. Makes it impossible to monitor my speed and try to maintain a minimum. The rest of the app still works fine.

I've been using this app for about six years now and recorded over three thousand miles on it. The last two weeks, the application no longer tracks distance or calories burned. I have repeatedly stopped and restarted both the app and my phone, as well as checking all my location and GPS settings. I'm trying one more time to reinstall this app. If it doesn't work, I'm changing to Strava. Well, I'm pleased to say, that today, 11-08-21 my app worked perfectly. Thanks to the team at MapMyRide!

The app failed to capture GPS information during parts of many of my trips. For the missing parts, it just draws a straight line on the map over the unknown segments. That means the route, distance, and calories calculations are wrong. The only thing it tells me correctly is the elapsed time, which I can figure in my head. Almost useless! Post Script: I learned you have to make an adjustment to your phone to keep the app from hibernating. Once I did that, it worked fine.

I love this app. I use it to track both cycling and walking. Setup for a workout is quick and easy. It keeps track of all the important indicators and it maps my location with other useful information during my workout. I love the overview calendar and the ability to edit details of the workout after the workout is saved.

Had an issue with the latest update, but it seems to have resolved itself. Still have an issue with the app insisting on including "intensity" as a parameter, no matter what I've done to delete it. I did reach out to support and they offered several suggestions, but none have worked. So, every time I start a workout, I need to customize the data all over again. If not for that stupidly aggravating annoyance, I'd give it 1 or 2 more stars.

Works great, been using it since 2011. Reliable, good information for me as a recreational cyclist and hiker...routes, mileage, averages, calories burned. Does suck up my battery, though, so I need to make sure I am fully charged before going out on long 40 + miles rides. Does not appear to connect to Fitbit directly, and I'm not sure that I need it to, but does connect to MyFitnessPal.

This app used to be one of my favorites, and I have used it for many years. However, recently the GPS has stopped working properly. Although the app begins recording my ride normally when launched, if I stop during the ride for any length of time the GPS seems to get lost and only restarts when I open the app and restart it manually. Unfortunately, I never know when the GPS stops working unless I look at see if it's still working properly. Consequently, the app is now completely useless to me.

I have been using this app for years, and it has been an absolute gift! Since the latest update, however, the app will stop registering my workout at random intervals, dropping the GPS on routes I have cycled many times before. I'm sure it will be fixed, but it's not really very useful right this moment.

Like that it tracks route, time, mileage. However, app recently started glitching within the past week. After I pause a workout or try to save, it gets stuck on save page & keeps going to that page. I have to close the app several times, discard saving workout multiple x's & somehow it goes back and I have to out-quick the app to pause & save again. The workout seems to eventually save, idk how it doesn't disappear? I have the latest version. Samsung 22+. Please fix!

Inconsistent, inaccurate and unreliable. The app frequently fails to track my route accurately while walking or biking, resulting in grossly inaccurate mileage. The app has ended my workouts twice without me pressing the 'pause' and 'hold to finish' buttons. Within a matter of just a few minutes the app announced I reached the 5 mile point on a walk... then 6 miles, then 7 miles and this continued for miles 8, 9, 10 and 11. Ultimately, the app added 4 miles to my workout that I did not walk.

Throughout the ride a pleasant female voice is periodicly saying total distance, split times, total time, etc. Love it. Added note:Two issues : 1 is heart rate logging is only on certain days. I log all the info in Spreadsheet. So I wish that was more consistent. 2 the elevation tracking has to be wrong. It can't be negative where I'm at. I'd rather have heart rate all the time and shut off elevation.

A very thorough app for those who want to keep track of their excersize plans and goals. It has all imaginable kinds of activities, allows to build your own excersize routine, does good job tracking bike rides and walks. You can set up goals which are pretty customizable. Separately, you can set up a plan for how you are going to achieve your goals. My only complaint is that it takes forever to load, probably because it stores data on the cloud - which came handy a few times, so no stars off

This app is great. The one thing I don't like is that it uses a bar graph to show your speed at set intervals rather than a line graph; This makes it hard to see where your rests are. The average speed includes any rests you took, so the stated average speed is less than what you were really going; this would have been solved if it just used a line graph and let you see your speed at manually set points/intervals. This app certainly meets, and passes some expectations given that it's free.

I used Endomondo for years and it was far superior until Under Armor bought it and shut it down. Map My Ride lacks the weekly totals and other cumulative things that Endomondo had. It also lacks the desk top view for viewing all of your stored work outs. I guess it's better than nothing. In one word, underwhelmed. When they try to sell you the upgrade you literally can't access your account. They hold YOUR information hostage!

Tried to use the app today and it pushed a 14 day free trial upgrade on me. There was no option to decline, so I finally decide to accept it and cancel later. Then it gave me an error message that it "could not download the product at this time." Despite this it wouldn't release my phone from the upgrade loop. When this app works it's great, but bugs like this make it not worth the trouble. It gets in the way of my work out, rather than supporting it.

First time using. Like it so far. Need to check total accuracy. This will help with logging my rides. I will check back in after a month. Peace out til' then. Follow o n report: I am impressed withnthe accuracy and functionality of the app. It is more accurate than my bike computer. Two thumbs up, three snaps in the Z-formation, and five stars.

App used to work, as of mid-Oct 2021 it stopped keeping track of mileage. It just stops recording mid-workout. I've opened a worthless ticket with UA as the suggestions were lame and already attempted, close other apps, restart phone etc. There is something wrong in the GPS interface with either this app or the connection this app is no longer making with the phone/operating system.

I like this app a lot. I primarily Mountain Bike and it does a good job of tracking distance and monitoring data however like most apps using GPS it does not account well for distance traveled when ascending/descending. For example one trail I ride often on an 8 mile loop it is usually 1/3 to 1/2 mile behind on actual distance traveled. It may do better with hiking or running because a person is not moving as fast up and down the hills.

Been using this app for tracking road and mountain bike rides since 2015. Great way to track progress on identical rides, and to 'total up' mileage for the year. The free service provides everything I need; ave speed, speed at mile intervals, total miles, time and maps showing route. Can even switch to satellite view. Highly recommended for new or experienced riders.

Like the features, but this app is really lousy at tracking distance. Went for a 12 mile ride, and recorded as 3, even though the entire 12 mi distance showed up on the map. (Distance verified by Google fit). Next ride of 18 miles showed up as 1.9 miles. Since it shows the distance on the map, can only conclude that there is a software glitch. Nonetheless, if it cannot accurately track my distance, speed and calc calories, what is the point of the app? Big fail!

Update 230119: I've been using this app continuously on a variety of bikes and rides. Works well for me with minimal intrusiveness. Auto pause works pretty well, and battery life on my phone is good. Update 181029. Using this for a while now, with Strava. Neither app meets 100% of what I want, so I use them together, hence 4 stars instead of 5. (Earlier) Worked well on my first ride. Didn't seem too bad on the battery, but I didn't leave the display on continuously.

Have always liked this app but it use to have kayaking listed as an activity but I can no longer find it in the list. Wish you would put it back in as rowing, canoeing, and paddling are not exactly the same thing, close maybe but not the same. The app does a good job of mapping where I go, the elevation is completely wrong but it never has been accurate on the water for some reason. But do not really care just kind of funny to look at. Works fine for mountain biking.

2019 update... it doesn't work anymore. it has turned a regular 19 mile ride into (variously) 16 miles, 2.5 miles. etc. Tried running a similar app in parallel today - the other app worked, map my ride did not (it's map shows me teleporting across the Hudson river). So, down to 1 star (can't do zero). May give it one more chance. and it's too bad - used for years and never before any complaint ** It works. Seem to get more accurate results that with strada, though don't know why that would be.

I've used map my ride for a few years to keep track of my workouts, mostly cycling. It does a great job of tracking routes which is helpful especially if your exiting and creating cycling routes. The GPS and compass integration into their map feature can also be used as a quide in case you get lost or off track, this is the case as long you get a strong gps signal. It's basic features are easy to navigate. The dashboard feature is also good see each weeks total workouts.

I have been using Map My Ride for over a year now, mostly for cycling plus a few runs and walks. It is perfect. It works well on my Pixel 4a phone. While riding the app announces mile splits, maps the route accurately, and gives elevation statistics among others. I definitely recommend it and don't want to ride without it now. Also, you can save rides if you choose.

there's a lot of great features. I'm no pro bike rider by any means for reference! but I love how precise it is on the distance and even shows a map when you complete your workout. it also great that it tells you your distance and time out loud at each mile so you don't have to stop and see how far you have gone. helped me really improve my time and distance because I like to try and beat my time a little bit each ride. hopefully this helps!

I love this app!! It works great, keep track of my ride as I'm going I can pause it when I stop to rest and then I can save it and track my progress from day-to-day or week-to-week no matter how you use it it's a great app!! Update: 5/30/22 I feel absolutely love this app!! It works so well and is very accurate and I like how it announces when you hit mile marker & time. Then when you're through you see the map of the route that you traveled and you can save your workout. I wouldn't use any app.

App used to work super well! Recently though, the GPS tracking has had some issues. When I start recording the GPS works no problem, but if I turn my phone off or even exit the app while it's still running, move a certain distance and then open it back up, the GPS jumps straight to where I'm at. It doesn't seem to be able to run the GPS in the background, which wasn't a problem previously. I've looked all over but is there something I'm missing?

Great app. I wanted an app to track my mileage on the bike trail. I loved how it told me every mile how far I'd gone and my average speed for the mile. After the last update, my app no longer works on my Pixel phone. I'm stuck with an orange screen and can't see my previous workouts. Ok, so it works great again. I had to uninstall it, shut my phone off, and reinstall it and it works again. I didn't lose my workouts either.

I haven't explored all the details of how it works, and if there is a paid version then I only have the free version, but it reports miles covered and speed and such, so it is pretty cool. I like how it maps it so you can see what you did and where. I'll add that you need to go into app settings to have it auto-detect pauses. We did a slow, casual bike ride with our daughter and it didn't pause when I stopped until I fixed that in settings, but after that, it did really well.

Used for 8 years. Used to be great, but in 2019 something really changed. No longer accurate with actual speed during ride, keeps running when paused. Have tried everything to remedy, contacted support, still no good. But after 8 years and all my data in it, hate to lose everything. Still hoping it gets fixed, but after 8 months of waiting, had to write this negative review.

I love the app I use it all the time it's fantastic for tracking workouts calories splits Paces everything. My go-to workout is cycling while listening to Audible perfect combination. The only thing I would say about this app is it would be nice if I could keep it up on my phone which is the Galaxy S6 Edge Plus while I'm riding so I can see the map and everything as I go without a draining the battery leaving the app open drains the battery a little too quickly for my liking that's the only complaint. I love that while I'm riding in the screen is turned off and I'm strictly focused on the exercise my audiobook that's playing in my ear through my headphones is paused while I get a mileage update and a split speed update from the app and I don't lose a place in the book or have to do anything that would have required me to take my hands away from the handlebars. Awesome app!

This app was awesome until the latest upgrade, but now the ads are horrendous. They send you to ads online and in the play store, and getting back to the app isn't always easy. I didn't mind exiting out of the ads on the last version, but this is too much. It's not worth the $30 they're asking so I'm looking for an alternative. Edit: they've fixed the way ads work so I've upped my rating.

I've used this app for years and despite a few bugs I've always loved it. Recently however, the app just stopped working properly. I have a note 9 and been having problems with tracking. The speed counter is terribly wrong, freezes at the same speed when you stop and pause it and at times keeps running regardless. I've uninstalled many times and now about to delete for good. Please fix this, I like to continue keeping track of my activities since I started cycling all in one place.

Great app! It doesn't quite work as a Bell dashboard, effectively it is better overall with the one exception that a dashboard has - mph at the very moment of movement, real time. Love it for increments of travel, mapped, elevation and speeds. Great database and stored workouts, comparisons with others. Just to think what the paid app might have, this review is on the free one : )

[Updated at end with what works to fix this] I've been using this app for over 6 years. Since upgrading to Oreo it is no longer capable of tracking my GPS. I've tried everything. I've followed all of their suggestions. It'll occasionally track a workout here and there (weeks apart), but generally it doesn't work anymore. [Update: Start your workout, click the app window switching button on your phone, hit the 3 dots, select lock. The app should work until unlocked].

I have the latest version as auto update is on. Lately, the app has been inconsistent in how it tracks my ride. Most days it's fine. Today it tracked my time for about 7 or 8 minutes, and showed calories burning, but the distance stayed at zero. Distance finally started tracking when I had ridden about a mile and a half. Because the time had already been running, the average speed when the app tracked 2 miles was 3.7 mph, when I know I was riding around 8 mph the whole time. Please fix this!

It's a decent app for beginners and casual cycling enthusiasts. It's definitely not as robust and lacks the depth of the social network (and volume of users) as Strava, which is what I really recommend for cyclists. The number of ads in this app is staggering, and it is becoming incredibly slow, especially when finishing and uploading the route. Most recently, the activities aren't even uploading until the next time the app starts up.

This app used to be great. It's terrible, now. Usually can't get a map at all. Sometimes, it has me riding through the lake. Distance is often off by more than a mile. Top speed can put me at 65mph or so. Voice usually doesn't work. Did all the things support suggested. Uninstalled and reinstalled more than once. Update: 4 stars now. Works a lot better than it used to.

It's supposed to be more accurate now. Well it's not. Sometimes it doesn't even turn on. Sometimes it gets stuck at 3.4 MPH, I'm pretty sure I'm riding faster than that, I can walk faster than that! Whatever you guys did, it didn't work. I recommend doing more beta testing this new version. I've already tried different GPS settings and permissions. Still doesn't work. I'm going to use Strava until you guys can get this fixed. Or please give the option to turn OFF GPS filtering! Thank you

I have the free version and its super easy to use to track my biking. It keeps times distance and even has metrics for tracking improvements. You can set weekly or daily or even monthly goals for tim, distance, calories etc. All that and its still simple to use. Theres sometimes a little glitch saving a picture but its not a problem. You just have to retrieve the pic from the gallery. Love this app and its kept me constantly riding for over a month.

Just used it for the first time. Seems to work as advertised so far. (previous rating was 4stars in April '17). - Update 6-5-2020 first time used this season. Not nearly as good. Can't find friends routes. My activity is not nearly as informative. Adds in every screen plus pop up adds that take over the whole screen, plus their own adds for the premium version. I already pay a premium for your athletic wear. I'll be looking at Strava or something else.

The app on phone works well...I wish it would auto detect start and ends of bike rides after the 1st start (like strava) bcs I sometimes forget to turn it off, or back on, as I shop on eŕands. What sucks, is the website later for editing, as there is no option to delete all points BETWEEN 2 points, as I always need to delete every up n down of grocery aisles manually, since as mentioned I forget to turn it off and it does not autodetect the difference between riding and walking speeds.

After a total of one ride, I'm happy with what I see so far. Update: I have been tracking my walking and riding for over a month now. This app is reliable and shows me everything I need to know. Update: I have been using this app for years now. The only thing that confounds me is how my Fitbit watch and the app disagree about distance and calories. The Fitnit has no GPS, so presumably they both use my phone's GPS. Anyway, no biggie. Map My Ride is still great in my book.
